steps.title: Identify the gerund phrase subject
steps.content: The strongest signal is the grammatical structure of the second clause (after the semicolon). The subject is “landing on one of the good spaces”—a gerund phrase, which is a noun form of a verb. Gerund phrases are singular by nature.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: A singular subject requires a singular verb, and “allows” is the only singular form here. The other choices are all non-singular forms that violate this agreement. A is correct.
